items.t: — Q2 rough output jumped to 7.8M ct even as the realised price fell 32% to $105/ct and the rough index dropped 16%; Q2 revenue −44% to $665M. Full-year guidance 21–26M ct, with Venetia paused in H2
— bullion defends $4,000 as Brent tops $100 and the 10-year yield reaches 4.70%; September rate-hike odds jump to 80% from 68% a week ago
— the Louvre's Apollo Gallery reopened July 22, nine months after the $100M royal-jewel heist; the jewels move to a secure vault and will no longer be shown. Four arrested, stones still missing
— the higher-quality emerald auction took $26.8M, selling 36 of 37 lots at $146.08/ct, 99% by weight; a 10% rupee slide since September is a headwind and the ruby sale is being held back
— June's RapNet index rose 4.2% for 0.30-ct and 1.3% for half-carat goods, and 0.4% at 3-ct, while the one-carat benchmark slipped 0.7%; inventory cuts, not demand, drive the small end
— BriteCo appraisal data puts lab-grown 73% below natural, up from 27% in 2019; as the discount widened, natural stones reclaimed 57.3% of US engagement rings
— New York High Jewelry made $31.4M, 95% sold, led by a $3.2M Graff 35-ct D ring; Phillips withdrew a 3.03-ct fancy pink after bids missed the reserve
— June exports rebounded 11.2% (FHS) and Swatch shares jumped, yet the group's H1 net profit was CHF 16M on CHF 3.12B of sales, half a percent
— Anglo American flags a negative H1 underlying result at De Beers as it presses the sale; reports name a Gareth Penny consortium as front-runner for the 85% stake
— the white metals sit quiet as gold slips: platinum near $1,628.63 and palladium near $1,274.96, little changed on the session
